name: PBIXRay MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server using PBIXRay to analyze Power BI (.pbix) files. Enables AI agents to inspect Power BI report internals — DAX measures, data models, relationships, data sources, and metadata — without requiring Power BI Desktop. Useful for Power BI governance, auditing, and documentation automation.
use_cases: Auditing Power BI report data models and DAX measures from governance agents
Documenting Power BI report structure and dependencies from documentation agents
Analyzing Power BI file internals for migration and optimization agents
Extracting Power BI metadata for catalog and lineage tracking agents
not_for: Rendering Power BI visuals (file analysis only, not live report rendering)
Teams without Power BI .pbix files to analyze
Real-time Power BI dashboard interaction (use Power BI REST API for that)
best_when: A Power BI developer or BI admin wants AI agents to audit, document, or analyze the internals of Power BI .pbix files — understanding data model structure, DAX complexity, and data source dependencies.
avoid_when: You need to interact with live Power BI reports or embed reports. PBIXRay analyzes the file format — not the live service.

auth.notes: No authentication — local file analysis tool. Reads .pbix files from local filesystem.

pricing.notes: Free open source using PBIXRay library.

performance.rate_limits: Local — bounded by file size and analysis complexity

known_agent_gotchas: .pbix files are binary Microsoft format — PBIXRay decodes them but coverage may be incomplete
Power BI file format can change with Power BI Desktop versions — verify version compatibility
Large .pbix files with complex models may take significant time to parse
Report metadata and credentials embedded in .pbix files are sensitive — handle with care
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local .pbix file analysis. Power BI files may contain embedded connection strings and data source credentials — review file contents before sharing. No external access.

security_score_components.security_notes: .pbix files may contain connection credentials. Local analysis only. No external access. Protect pbix files accordingly.
